description: A broad category of filled rolls wrapped in thin pastry or rice paper. Depending on the style, they may be fried until crisp or served fresh with herbs and dipping sauce.
preparation_steps: Prepare the filling by cooking or seasoning the vegetables and any protein.
Wrap the filling in pastry or rice paper.
Either fry until crisp or serve fresh depending on the style.
Serve with an appropriate dipping sauce.

nutrition.calories: Varies from low to moderate for fresh rolls and moderate to high for fried rolls
nutrition.fat: Low for fresh versions; higher for fried versions

health_advice.benefits: Can include vegetables and lean protein, especially in fresh versions.
health_advice.risks: Fried versions may be high in oil and sodium depending on the filling and sauces.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Choose fresh or lightly cooked versions and include plenty of vegetables.

dietary_notes.note: Suitability depends on wrapper type and filling. Rice paper rolls are often gluten-free, while wheat wrappers are not.
